capabilities: A modular library system must clearly separate public APIs from internal modules to enhance maintainability and scalability.

procedure.action: Conduct a requirements analysis by gathering input from stakeholders and documenting use cases; prioritize features based on impact and feasibility using a MoSCoW prioritization matrix using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Specify the public API by listing all externally accessible functions and data structures; ensure compliance with OpenAPI Specification for consistent documentation and versioning by specifying schema, constraints, and invariants in machine-readable form; include at least one falsifiable acceptance criterion where error_rate < 0.001.

Extract internal modules by decomposing the system into functional components; use Domain Driven Design to align modules with business domains and ensure clear boundaries by scanning each candidate against the detection ruleset; assert match_score > 0.7 for selected records; log identified_count and unmatched_count to detection_log.

Instantiate a modular design by applying the Aggregator Pattern to consolidate related modules; ensure each module adheres to the Single Responsibility Principle to minimize interdependencies by instantiating the resource with the specified configuration; assert resource_id != null and resource_state = 'active'; log creation_duration_ms to audit_log.
Invoke automated tests for each module using a framework like JUnit or PyTest; ensure test coverage exceeds 80% for critical paths and public API endpoints.

summary: Ensure maintainability and scalability by designing modular library systems with distinct public APIs and internal modules.
title: Modular Library System Design

failureConditions: Decreased maintainability when public APIs and internal modules are not clearly separated, causing tight coupling and complex dependencies.
Scalability issues when internal modules are exposed directly, leading to unintentional usage and increased risk of breaking changes.
Increased defect rate when automated tests are insufficient, causing undetected errors in critical paths and public API endpoints.
metrics: all public API endpoints return expected results
successConditions: run integration tests; assert all public API endpoints return expected results; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
